---
title: "Install"
---

{{< include _setup.qmd >}}

## Python

```sh
git clone https://github.com/rieszreg/rieszboost.git
cd rieszboost
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install -e python/
```

`xgboost` requires OpenMP. On macOS:

```sh
brew install libomp
```

Linux distributions (and GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest) ship `libgomp1`; no extra step is needed.

### Verify

::: {.panel-tabset group="lang"}

## Python

```{python}
import rieszboost
from rieszboost import RieszBooster, ATE
print("rieszboost", "OK")
```

## R

```{r}
print("rieszboost (R wrapper) OK")
```

:::

## R wrapper (optional)

The R package is an R6 wrapper that calls the Python implementation through [reticulate](https://rstudio.github.io/reticulate/). It does not require any separate algorithmic dependency.

First-time setup:

1. Install the R package dependencies:
   ```r
   install.packages(c("reticulate", "R6"))
   ```
2. Load the R wrapper. For this guide we dev-load it from the source tree:
   ```r
   pkgload::load_all("r/rieszboost")
   ```
3. Point reticulate at the Python venv:
   ```r
   use_python_rieszboost(".venv/bin/python")
   ```
   Or set the environment variable before R starts:
   ```sh
   export RETICULATE_PYTHON=/path/to/rieszboost/.venv/bin/python
   ```

The R API exposes every built-in estimand, both losses, both backends, and the full `RieszBooster` interface. The symbolic tracer for custom `m()` is Python-only; see [r-interface](r-interface.qmd) for how to call it from R.

## Tests

```{.bash}
.venv/bin/python -m pytest python/tests -v

Rscript -e '
  Sys.setenv(RETICULATE_PYTHON = file.path(getwd(), ".venv/bin/python"))
  pkgload::load_all("r/rieszboost")
  testthat::test_dir("r/rieszboost/tests/testthat")
'
```
